@@ -77,7 +77,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-4rsog","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347: SPMC send_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel on slow-consumer backpressure","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpmcProducer::send_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits shared.not_full[full_idx].notified() at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347. The docs promise pre-cancel and cancel during blocked notified.await return Err(value), but a cancellation after the future has suspended does not wake the Notify. If one consumer queue stays full and no consumer drains after cancellation, the producer can hang indefinitely despite the caller Cx being cancelled.

Expected fix direction: use the same select-race/cx-aware notify helper as the SPSC sender; add a regression where one SPMC consumer is full, send_with_cx blocks, cx is cancelled, and no consumer drain occurs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:23.486857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:43.374739Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:43.374522Z","close_reason":"Fixed spmc send_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4ua6b","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262: SPSC recv_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_empty","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpscConsumer::recv_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits a plain asupersync Notify at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262. The docs state that a cancel during the blocked wait breaks out and returns None, but a mid-flight cancellation does not wake this notify wait. If the queue is empty and no producer sends or closes after cancellation, the receiver stays suspended. This is especially risky because runtime persistence currently waits on the non-cx recv path while shutdown_flag only gets checked after a message arrives.

Expected fix direction: race not_empty.notified() against a cx-cancel watcher or route through a common cx-aware notify wait; add a regression with empty queue, blocked recv_with_cx, then cancel without send/close.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:16.399653Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:40.241522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:40.241292Z","close_reason":"Fixed spsc recv_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5490a","title":"[golden-artifacts] TOON encoder: format=toon emits across robot+MCP, no exact-bytes corpus pinning the rendered output","description":"## The gap

ft's robot mode and MCP layer support `format=toon` for token-
efficient AI-to-AI output (per README's TL;DR: 'TOON-compact —
use this when piping to an AI'). Two existing tests:

- `tests/proptest_toon_roundtrip.rs` (21 KB) — proves
  `serialize → deserialize == identity` for arbitrary inputs.
- `tests/wa_mission_toon_mcp_conformance.rs` and
  `wa_tx_toon_mcp_conformance.rs` — assert TOON output for specific
  mission/tx envelopes parses back into the expected JSON shape.
- `tests/golden_pane_state_toon.rs` — pins ONE TOON output (pane
  state). 5.6 KB.

Property tests enforce shape invariants. The mission/tx tests
enforce parses-back-to-JSON. **Neither pins the exact rendered
TOON bytes for the broader robot envelope corpus.**

`grep -lE "toon.*golden\|TOON_GOLDEN\|to_toon.*golden"
tests/*.rs` returns zero hits beyond `golden_pane_state_toon.rs`.

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

The README's TL;DR sells TOON specifically as 'token-efficient AI-
to-AI output'. The encoded *bytes* are the value proposition — a
new field on a robot envelope that adds 50 tokens per response,
or a re-ordering that makes the output less compressible by an
AI's vocabulary, breaks the entire 'token-efficient' contract
silently. Property tests don't catch this because they preserve
roundtripability, not byte-economy.

Concrete drift hazards a corpus would catch:
- TOON encoder version change reorders fields, blowing up token
  counts.
- A robot tool gains an `Option<String>` field that serializes as
  `key=null,` even when None — invisible in JSON, present in TOON,
  uses tokens.
- Whitespace drift between major TOON versions silently hits the
  full robot/MCP surface.

## Suggested implementation

Mirror the structure of `tests/golden_robot_envelope/` (which I
shipped runtime-validation for in ft-5ikbd) but for TOON output:

1. **Corpus** at `tests/fixtures/toon_envelope_corpus/<tool>.toon`
   with one frozen TOON rendering per robot tool that pins
   meaningful output (state, get-text, search, send, mission_state,
   tx_run, tx_show, …).
2. **Generator** (or test-side fixture builder) that produces a
   deterministic input envelope per fixture and renders it via the
   production TOON encoder. The same input is checked into
   `tests/fixtures/toon_envelope_corpus/<tool>.input.json` so the
   regeneration command is auditable.
3. **Test driver**
   `tests/conformance_toon_envelope_corpus.rs`:
   - For each `<tool>.input.json`, render via the production TOON
     encoder, scrub timestamps/elapsed_ms/durations, diff against
     `<tool>.toon`.
   - Bonus: assert byte-count is within ±10% of a stored
     `<tool>.byte_budget.txt` so regressions in token-economy
     surface as test failures (not just silent bloat).
4. **UPDATE_GOLDENS workflow** — same pattern as the existing
   `tests/golden/` infrastructure (PROVENANCE.md row, diff review).

## Out of scope

- Comparing TOON to JSON byte counts (token economy is the focus,
  but cross-format comparison is a separate analysis).
- Reranking / weighting of which tools are highest-value to pin
  (start with the README-enumerated robot family).

## Acceptance criteria

- ≥10 `<tool>.toon` goldens across the robot envelope family.
- A new field on a robot tool's response struct that serializes
  as TOON makes the corresponding golden fail with a precise diff.
- Byte-budget assertion (or equivalent) flags >10% regression.
- PROVENANCE.md row records the encoder version pinned at
  generation.

## References

- `tests/proptest_toon_roundtrip.rs` (existing property coverage)
- `tests/golden_pane_state_toon.rs` (the existing single TOON
  golden — template for the corpus expansion)
- README §TL;DR (TOON's stated value proposition)
- ft-5ikbd (sibling: JSON envelope schema validator that established
  the corpus directory pattern)

Filed by cc_1 golden-artifacts audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:27:38.550302Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:48:10.434142Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 golden-artifacts audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:27:38.550302Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:57:22.407021Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T09:57:22.406860Z","close_reason":"added exact-byte TOON robot/MCP goldens and conformance test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843379Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 05c8d36b (Option 2 from the bead). Semantic invariant polls>=1 remains enforced in assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477 (runs against the live envelope at :733, BEFORE canonicalization). canonicalize() at :480 now zeroes polls alongside now/elapsed_ms/captured_at so the byte-stable golden only freezes structural presence, not scheduler-timing-dependent count. wa_wait_for.json updated to polls:0 to match. Only golden in the tree carrying a polls key is wa_wait_for.json, so the canonicalize rule is unambiguous and cannot mask a real regression in another tool's envelope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-5hp7p","title":"[config-types][test] extracted crate tests miss toml dev-dependency","description":"frankenterm-core-config-types inline tests use toml::to_string_pretty/from_str in tuning_config.rs, but the extracted crate manifest only declared serde. cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types failed with E0433 unresolved crate toml until the dev-dependency is restored.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:05.296081Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.437023Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.436733Z","close_reason":"Added the missing toml dev-dependency so cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types passes. Landed in 08e48279.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -210,7 +210,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-c1lfg","title":"[mcp] wa.mission_state mission_state filter miss still returns a success mission payload","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:3696-3725, wa.mission_state checks params.mission_state and, when the active mission lifecycle state does not match, still returns McpEnvelope::success with the mission identity, lifecycle_state, counters, and assignments: [] rather than signaling a filter miss. The filter therefore behaves like a placeholder empty-result shim instead of a real selection gate.\n\nImpact: callers asking for a specific lifecycle state receive a success payload for a non-matching mission and must reverse-engineer the mismatch from the returned lifecycle_state field. This makes the advertised filter semantically misleading and complicates conformance/golden expectations.\n\nFix direction: either treat mission_state as a real filter that returns an explicit no-match shape/flag, or reject the request with a targeted error when the active mission does not satisfy the requested lifecycle state. Add coverage for the mismatch path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:25.019931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:51:18.381660Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:51:18.380953Z","close_reason":"Fixed in cec75f71: wa.mission_state now rejects lifecycle-state filter misses instead of returning a success payload for the wrong mission; added mission_state mismatch regression in mcp_tools.rs. Verification partial: local cargo lanes hung after compile/warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-c7dft","title":"runtime rename left E2E guards probing deleted runtime_compat.rs","description":"After ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and the live source file is runtime_async.rs, several executable E2E guard scripts still grep crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs. Those lanes fail before validating the runtime surface because the file no longer exists. Update executable guard scripts to probe runtime_async.rs while leaving historical docs alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:49:54.267240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:54:02.530711Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:54:02.530222Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4360d234: E2E runtime guard scripts now target runtime_async.rs and ft-22x4r validation passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
-{"id":"ft-cke6c","title":"[test-blocker] proptest_patterns_metamorphic uses stale TriggerCategoryCounts map API","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core toon currently compiles past the TOON golden test and Tantivy test API fixes, then fails in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs: chunked_trigger_counts still returns HashMap<TriggerCategory,u64> while ScanPipeline now exposes TriggerCategoryCounts. Errors: E0308 at line 370 and E0277/PartialEq mismatches around lines 471, 534, 577.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:09.843723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:09.843723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-cke6c","title":"[test-blocker] cargo test toon blocked by stale Tantivy and TriggerCategoryCounts tests","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core toon is blocked before completing the TOON-filtered suite by pre-existing test API drift outside ft-5490a. First observed failures: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tantivy_integration_tests.rs implements old IndexLookup methods has_event_id/get_log_offset and omits IntegrityCheckConfig.batch_size. A local exploratory repair then exposed a second stale API in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs where chunked_trigger_counts still returns HashMap<TriggerCategory,u64> while ScanPipeline now exposes TriggerCategoryCounts (E0308 at line 370, E0277/PartialEq around lines 471/534/577).  {"id":"ft-cqhfe","title":"[ft-gkqej/3] Update multi-pass-bug-hunting / mock-code-finder skill templates to emit SHA-anchored frontmatter","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:57:57.639942Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765357Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765142Z","close_reason":"Appended an 'Audit Marker Output (SHA-anchored frontmatter)' section to both /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/multi-pass-bug-hunting/SKILL.md (between Documentation Template and Convergence Criteria) and /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/mock-code-finder/SKILL.md (between Lessons From Real Sessions and Anti-Patterns). Each section: (1) shows the required YAML frontmatter shape (name / description / type / swept_at_sha / sweep_paths / stale_after_commits / findings_summary), (2) explains how to populate each field — captures swept_at_sha via 'git rev-parse HEAD' BEFORE the sweep starts so subsequent fix commits don't shift the anchor, lists resolved sweep_paths not globs, and picks stale_after_commits per churn class (storage/runtime: 30, mux/codec/vendored: 20, patterns/config/DSL: 5), (3) references the audit_marker_doctor.sh helper (frankenterm scripts/, ft-nedq3) and its CI-friendly exit codes 0/1/2, (4) cites the failure mode that motivated the convention — the 2026-04-20 sweep markers describing themselves as 'clean' while their swept paths quietly accumulated 24/91/154 commits.  {"id":"ft-cui2w","title":"[test][caut-types] add unit tests to newly-extracted leaf crate (ft-2z15d follow-on)","description":"ft-2z15d landed crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/ at 0d5cba9f with the type definitions for CautService, CautUsage, CautRefresh, CautAccountUsage, CautError, plus the is_openai_slug / is_anthropic_slug / is_google_slug helpers. The crate ships with ZERO tests — extracting types from a parent crate into a leaf is the moment to pin behaviour at the new boundary, otherwise a future refactor on the parent's now-tinier caut.rs (which retained the IO-bound CautClient + parsers) won't notice if the leaf's slug parsing or serde alias fields drift.\n\nScope: add a #[cfg(test)] module to crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/src/lib.rs covering:\n  - CautService::as_str / provider_arg / supported_cli_inputs roundtrip\n  - CautService::from_cli_input on every supported slug + a few invalid ones\n  - is_openai_slug / is_anthropic_slug / is_google_slug case-insensitive + whitespace-trim behaviour\n  - CautError Display strings match the #[error(...)] declarations for every variant (incl. structured ones with placeholders)\n  - CautError serde JSON roundtrip\n  - CautUsage / CautRefresh / CautAccountUsage serde roundtrip preserves the camelCase alias fields (percentRemaining, limitHours, resetAt, tokensUsed, tokensRemaining, tokensLimit) AND the #[serde(flatten)] extra HashMap captures unknown keys\n\nNon-goals:\n  - Test the in-core IO surface (CautClient, parse_json) — those stay with frankenterm-core::caut and are covered by the parent crate's existing tests / proptest_caut.rs.\n\nSister bead: ft-8cg6y's frankenterm-core-cass-types/ has the same gap; that bead can be filed separately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:11.048858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:28:01.505447Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:28:01.505200Z","close_reason":"Shipped at 2d0fb9f7: 12 unit tests in crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/src/lib.rs covering CautService (as_str/provider_arg/from_cli_input/Display), slug helpers (case-insensitive + whitespace-trim + cross-helper isolation), CautError (Display matches thiserror #[error] strings + serde roundtrip for every variant), and CautUsage/CautRefresh/CautAccountUsage (camelCase aliases, #[serde(flatten)] extras, roundtrip, default). 12/12 pass in 7.02s.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["caut","leaf-crate","testing"]}
